verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted but its latent complex (LAP + mature TGF-β1) is covalently tethered to cell surfaces via GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33 on Tregs, platelets, and myeloid cells through disulfide bonds, forming a wash-resistant surface-displayed latent complex. This surface-anchored LAP/TGF-β1 is a validated therapeutic target (e.g., anti-GARP:TGF-β1 antibodies). Membrane-bound latent TGF-β1 is therefore accessible from the extracellular face via stable covalent attachment to a TM partner, not merely reversible recruitment.
